title: morus alba subvar. rosea
intro: Morus alba subvar. rosea, also known as the White Mulberry, is a variant of the White Mulberry tree (Morus alba), native to Asia and widely cultivated for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The bark and roots of the plant are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. The medicinal properties of Morus alba subvar. rosea include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities, which can help to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, can be used to treat a range of health conditions, and are often consumed as tea or taken as capsules or tablets.
